bibliographic_data.title: TOI-1685 b is a Hot Rocky Super-Earth: Updates to the Stellar and Planet Parameters of a Popular JWST Cycle 2 Target
bibliographic_data.abstract: We present an updated characterization of the TOI-1685 planetary system, which consists of a Pb = 0.69 day USP super-Earth planet orbiting a nearby (d = 37.6 pc) M2.5V star (TIC 28900646, 2MASS J04342248+4302148). This planet was previously featured in two contemporaneous discovery papers, but the best-fit planet mass, radius, and bulk density values were discrepant allowing it to be interpreted either as a hot, bare rock or a 50% H2O / 50% MgSiO3 water world. TOI-1685 b will be observed in three independent JWST cycle two programs, two of which assume the planet is a water world while the third assumes that it is a hot rocky planet. Here we include a refined stellar classification with a focus on addressing the host star’s metallicity, an updated planet radius measurement that includes two sectors of TESS data and multi-color photometry from a variety of ground-based facilities, and a more accurate dynamical mass measurement from a combined CARMENES, IRD, and MAROON-X radial velocity data set. We find that the star is very metal-rich ([Fe/H] ≃+0.3) and that the planet is systematically smaller, lower mass, and higher density than initially reported, with new best-fit parameters of Rpl= 1.468 +0.050 −0.051 R⊕and Mpl= 3.03+0.33 −0.32 M⊕. These results fall in between the previously derived values and suggest that TOI-1685 b is a hot, rocky planet with an Earth-like density (ρpl= 5.3 ± 0.8 g cm−3, or 0.96 ρ⊕), high equilibrium temperature (Teq = 1062 ± 27 K) and negligible volatiles, rather than a water world.

core_content.novelty_claim: The paper presents updated stellar and planetary parameters for TOI-1685 b, resolving discrepancies in previous measurements to suggest the planet is a hot rocky super-Earth.
core_content.key_methodology: The authors used new precise radial velocity measurements from MAROON-X, additional ground-based photometry from MuSCAT, and an additional sector of TESS data. They also refined the stellar classification focusing on metallicity.
outcomes: A refined stellar classification with a very metal-rich star ([Fe/H] ≃+0.3)
An updated planet radius measurement (Rpl= 1.468 +0.050 −0.051 R⊕) and mass measurement (Mpl= 3.03+0.33 −0.32 M⊕)
A more accurate interpretation of TOI-1685 b as a hot rocky planet with an Earth-like density, high equilibrium temperature, and negligible volatiles
core_content.full_text_markdown: The paper updates the characterization of the TOI-1685 planetary system. The authors use new data from TESS, radial velocity measurements, and ground-based photometry to refine the stellar and planetary parameters. They find that the star is very metal-rich ([Fe/H] ≃+0.3) and that the planet has a smaller radius (Rpl= 1.468 +0.050 −0.051 R⊕) and lower mass (Mpl= 3.03+0.33 −0.32 M⊕). These results suggest TOI-1685 b is a hot rocky planet with an Earth-like density, high equilibrium temperature, and negligible volatiles.
